datasets.name: mme_signaling_events
datasets.description: MME signaling events including attach procedures, TAU, handoffs, and session management. High-volume operational data from all MME hosts across regions. Includes detailed procedure outcomes and timing.
datasets.cardinality_notes: Multiple mme_host values (2-5) share the same (cluster_id, datacenter) combination to enable split-brain detection. Each MME processes 500-2000 events per hour.

datasets.name: diameter_transactions
datasets.description: HSS diameter protocol transactions for authentication and subscriber profile operations. Includes S6a interface messages between MME and HSS with detailed result codes and timing.

datasets.name: handoff_events
datasets.description: Cell handoff events from X2AP and S1-AP interfaces. Tracks handoff attempts, successes, and failures between cells with detailed failure reasons.

datasets.name: security_events
datasets.description: Security events from SS7 firewall, GTP firewall, and authentication failures. Includes attack detection, rogue access attempts, and fraud indicators.

datasets.name: subscriber_sessions
datasets.description: Active subscriber session snapshots from MME context tables. Tracks session establishment, modifications, and terminations with QoS details.

datasets.name: mme_hosts
datasets.description: MME host inventory with configuration, capacity, and regional assignment. Used for enriching signaling events with MME context.

datasets.name: cells
datasets.description: Cell tower inventory including eNodeB/gNodeB base stations with geographic location, technology type, and capacity metrics.

datasets.name: hss_nodes
datasets.description: HSS database node inventory with replication topology and capacity information. Used for enriching diameter transactions.

datasets.name: customer_segments
datasets.description: Customer segment definitions with ARPU, SLA terms, and business priority for revenue impact calculations.

queries.name: Core Network Split-Brain Detection with Multi-MME Context Conflict
queries.pain_point: Split-brain conditions where multiple MMEs claim ownership of same subscriber contexts cause signaling storms and session establishment failures, overwhelming diameter routing agents and HSS capacity. Current tools cannot detect duplicate transaction IDs or abnormal signaling patterns indicating split-brain scenarios.
queries.description: Detects split-brain conditions by identifying clusters where multiple MME hosts are processing signaling for the same IMSI within short time windows, indicating duplicate subscriber context ownership. Uses statistical analysis to identify abnormal multi-MME activity patterns that precede signaling storms.
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_events | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 2 hours | LOOKUP JOIN mme_hosts ON mme_host | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS unique_mmes = COUNT_DISTINCT(mme_host), total_events = COUNT(*), unique_subscribers = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi_hash) BY time_bucket, cluster_id, mme_hosts.datacenter | WHERE unique_mmes >= 2 | INLINESTATS avg_mmes = AVG(unique_mmes), p95_mmes = PERCENTILE(unique_mmes, 95) BY cluster_id | EVAL split_brain_severity = CASE(unique_mmes >= 4, "CRITICAL", unique_mmes >= 3, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP cluster_id, split_brain_severity, unique_mmes, unique_subscribers, total_events, time_bucket, mme_hosts.datacenter, mme_hosts.region | SORT unique_mmes DESC | LIMIT 20

queries.name: HSS Authentication Transaction Failure Analysis with Database Node Correlation
queries.pain_point: HSS database replication lag and corruption cause authentication failures affecting subscriber attach procedures, but current monitoring only tracks database-level metrics without correlating actual authentication transaction success rates with specific HSS node performance.
queries.description: Analyzes HSS authentication transaction success rates (AIR command code) correlated with specific HSS nodes to identify database replication issues or node-specific failures. Uses z-score analysis to detect abnormal response times indicating database query performance degradation.
queries.esql_query: FROM diameter_transactions | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 4 hours AND command_code == "AIR" | LOOKUP JOIN hss_nodes ON hss_node | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS auth_attempts = COUNT(*), auth_failures = COUNT(*) WHERE transaction_success == false, avg_response_ms = AVG(response_time_ms), p95_response_ms = PERCENTILE(response_time_ms, 95) BY time_bucket, hss_node, hss_nodes.node_role, hss_nodes.datacenter | EVAL success_rate_pct = ((auth_attempts - auth_failures) / auth_attempts) * 100 | INLINESTATS avg_success = AVG(success_rate_pct), stddev_success = STD_DEV(success_rate_pct), avg_latency = AVG(avg_response_ms), stddev_latency = STD_DEV(avg_response_ms) BY hss_nodes.datacenter | EVAL success_z_score = (success_rate_pct - avg_success) / COALESCE(stddev_success, 1), latency_z_score = (avg_response_ms - avg_latency) / COALESCE(stddev_latency, 1) | WHERE success_rate_pct < 98 OR latency_z_score > 2.5 | EVAL health_status = CASE(success_rate_pct < 95, "CRITICAL", success_rate_pct < 98, "DEGRADED", "WARNING") | KEEP hss_node, health_status, success_rate_pct, auth_failures, p95_response_ms, time_bucket, hss_nodes.node_role, hss_nodes.datacenter, hss_nodes.replication_master | SORT success_rate_pct ASC | LIMIT 25

queries.name: Cell Handoff Cascade Failure Pattern Detection with Geographic Clustering
queries.pain_point: Cell tower handoff failures cascade across multiple adjacent cells causing widespread dropped calls and data session interruptions, but current vendor-specific monitoring systems operate in silos making it impossible to identify cascading handoff failures across multiple cell sites.
queries.description: Identifies cascade failure patterns where a single problematic cell causes handoff failures to multiple neighboring cells. Analyzes handoff success rates for cell pairs and detects when 3+ target cells sharing the same source cell show degraded handoff performance, indicating a source cell issue.
queries.esql_query: FROM handoff_events | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 6 hours | LOOKUP JOIN cells AS source_cells ON source_cell_id = source_cells.cell_id | LOOKUP JOIN cells AS target_cells ON target_cell_id = target_cells.cell_id | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS total_handoffs = COUNT(*), failed_handoffs = COUNT(*) WHERE handoff_result == "failure", unique_target_cells = COUNT_DISTINCT(target_cell_id) BY time_bucket, source_cell_id, source_cells.region, source_cells.vendor | EVAL handoff_success_rate = ((total_handoffs - failed_handoffs) / total_handoffs) * 100 | WHERE handoff_success_rate < 90 AND unique_target_cells >= 3 | EVAL cascade_severity = CASE(handoff_success_rate < 70, "CRITICAL", handoff_success_rate < 85, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP source_cell_id, cascade_severity, handoff_success_rate, failed_handoffs, unique_target_cells, time_bucket, source_cells.region, source_cells.vendor | SORT failed_handoffs DESC | LIMIT 20

queries.name: Proactive MME Attach Procedure Degradation Detection with Multi-Dimensional Analysis
queries.pain_point: Network Operations Center relies on customer complaints and help desk ticket volume to identify network degradation, resulting in MTTD exceeding 15-20 minutes. Need to detect attach procedure failures 10-15 minutes before help desk is overwhelmed by identifying abnormal signaling patterns.
queries.description: Detects attach procedure degradation using statistical z-score analysis across MME hosts and cells. Identifies abnormal failure patterns 10-15 minutes before customer impact by comparing current attach success rates against baseline patterns, with drill-down to specific reject causes and affected cells.
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_events | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 4 hours AND procedure_type == "attach" | LOOKUP JOIN mme_hosts ON mme_host | LOOKUP JOIN cells ON cell_id | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(15 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS attach_attempts = COUNT(*), attach_failures = COUNT(*) WHERE procedure_result == "failure", unique_cells = COUNT_DISTINCT(cell_id) BY time_bucket, mme_host, mme_hosts.region, cells.technology | EVAL success_rate_pct = ((attach_attempts - attach_failures) / attach_attempts) * 100 | INLINESTATS avg_success = AVG(success_rate_pct), stddev_success = STD_DEV(success_rate_pct), p95_success = PERCENTILE(success_rate_pct, 95) BY mme_hosts.region | EVAL z_score = (success_rate_pct - avg_success) / COALESCE(stddev_success, 1) | WHERE success_rate_pct < 95 OR z_score < -2.5 | EVAL alert_severity = CASE(success_rate_pct < 90, "CRITICAL", success_rate_pct < 95, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP mme_host, alert_severity, success_rate_pct, attach_failures, unique_cells, time_bucket, mme_hosts.region, cells.technology, z_score | SORT success_rate_pct ASC | LIMIT 25

queries.name: SS7 Security Attack Detection with Semantic Pattern Analysis and Subscriber Impact
queries.pain_point: SS7 protocol exploitation for location tracking, SMS interception, and fraud attacks from rogue operators are detected too late (24-48 hours via billing systems). Need real-time detection of SS7 attacks correlated with network performance impact and affected subscriber counts.
queries.description: Detects SS7 security attacks using semantic analysis of attack descriptions to identify sophisticated exploitation patterns. Aggregates attacks by source network and type, calculates affected subscriber counts, and prioritizes based on severity and blocking success. Enables 5-minute detection vs 24-48 hour delay.
queries.esql_query: FROM security_events | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 24 hours AND event_type == "ss7_attack" AND SEMANTIC(attack_description, "location tracking SMS interception fraud roaming exploitation") | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS attack_count = COUNT(*), blocked_count = COUNT(*) WHERE blocked == true, affected_subscribers = COUNT_DISTINCT(target_imsi_hash), unique_sources = COUNT_DISTINCT(source_global_title) BY time_bucket, attack_type, source_network, severity | EVAL block_rate_pct = (blocked_count / attack_count) * 100 | WHERE attack_count >= 5 | EVAL threat_level = CASE(severity == "critical" AND block_rate_pct < 90, "ACTIVE_THREAT", severity == "critical", "BLOCKED_CRITICAL", severity == "high", "HIGH_RISK", "MONITORED") | KEEP attack_type, threat_level, attack_count, affected_subscribers, block_rate_pct, time_bucket, source_network, severity, unique_sources | SORT attack_count DESC | LIMIT 30

queries.name: Revenue Impact Calculation for Network Incidents with Customer Segment Analysis
queries.pain_point: Network outages directly impact revenue and trigger SLA penalties, but current reporting cannot quickly quantify business impact or identify which customer segments are affected. A single MME failure affecting 10,000 subscribers for 30 minutes represents $15,000-25,000 in lost revenue but takes hours to calculate manually.
queries.description: Calculates real-time revenue impact of network incidents by analyzing failed sessions, correlating with customer segment ARPU, and computing estimated revenue loss and SLA penalty exposure. Provides executive-level business impact metrics within minutes of incident detection for rapid decision-making.
queries.esql_query: FROM subscriber_sessions | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 6 hours AND session_result == "failure" | LOOKUP JOIN customer_segments ON customer_segment = customer_segments.segment_id | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp), incident_duration_hours = session_duration_seconds / 3600.0 | STATS affected_subscribers = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi_hash), failed_sessions = COUNT(*), avg_duration_hours = AVG(incident_duration_hours) BY time_bucket, mme_host, customer_segments.segment_name, customer_segments.monthly_arpu_usd, customer_segments.sla_penalty_per_hour_usd | EVAL hourly_arpu = monthly_arpu_usd / 730.0, estimated_revenue_loss = affected_subscribers * hourly_arpu * avg_duration_hours, estimated_sla_penalty = affected_subscribers * sla_penalty_per_hour_usd * avg_duration_hours, total_financial_impact = estimated_revenue_loss + estimated_sla_penalty | WHERE affected_subscribers >= 100 | EVAL impact_severity = CASE(total_financial_impact > 10000, "CRITICAL", total_financial_impact > 5000, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP mme_host, impact_severity, affected_subscribers, estimated_revenue_loss, estimated_sla_penalty, total_financial_impact, time_bucket, customer_segments.segment_name | SORT total_financial_impact DESC | LIMIT 20

queries.name: Subscriber Service Request Failure Root Cause Analysis with Device and Cell Correlation
queries.pain_point: UE-initiated service request failures cause poor user experience but root cause identification is difficult - could be radio issues, core network congestion, or device problems. Need deep-dive analysis to optimize network resource allocation and identify problematic device models or congested cells.
queries.description: Performs root cause analysis of service request failures by correlating failure patterns with cell capacity metrics and procedure latency. Identifies dominant failure causes (paging failure, RRC timeout, MME reject) and pinpoints capacity-constrained cells or technology-specific issues requiring targeted optimization.
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_events | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 12 hours AND procedure_type == "service_request" | LOOKUP JOIN cells ON cell_id | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS total_requests = COUNT(*), failed_requests = COUNT(*) WHERE procedure_result == "failure", avg_latency_ms = AVG(procedure_latency_ms), p95_latency_ms = PERCENTILE(procedure_latency_ms, 95) BY time_bucket, cell_id, cells.region, cells.technology, reject_cause | EVAL success_rate_pct = ((total_requests - failed_requests) / total_requests) * 100 | WHERE success_rate_pct < 95 | KEEP cell_id, success_rate_pct, failed_requests, reject_cause, p95_latency_ms, time_bucket, cells.region, cells.technology | SORT failed_requests DESC | LIMIT 30
